name: go_ner_benchmark_en_and_mixed
description: English and mixed-language subset extracted from benchmark_go_ner_200

cases.text: The kinase activity was significantly elevated in drought-stressed rice seedlings.
Chloroplast development is tightly coupled to photosynthesis efficiency under high light.
Cell division was accelerated in the meristematic zone following cytokinin treatment.
Signal transduction cascades mediate the plant response to drought and salinity stress.
Oxidative phosphorylation efficiency is impaired in plants with dysfunctional mitochondria.
Translation efficiency decreased in mutant lines lacking ribosomal assembly factors.
DNA repair pathways are activated upon UV irradiation to preserve genomic integrity.
Protein phosphorylation by MAP kinases initiates downstream defense signaling cascades.
Response to stress genes were highly upregulated in rice leaves under heat shock conditions.
Transmembrane transport of potassium ions is critical for stomatal opening and closure.
Cell wall organization is essential for maintaining structural integrity during plant elongation.
Mitochondrion inheritance is mediated by interactions between mitochondria and the cytoskeleton.
ATP synthesis coupled proton transport drives the mitochondrial electrochemical proton gradient.
Mitochondrial genome maintenance requires RecA-like recombinases in higher plant cells.
Kinase activity and signal transduction are tightly linked nodes in the MAPK cascade.
Cell division and cell wall organization are coordinately regulated during plant cytokinesis.
Translation and protein phosphorylation collaboratively regulate protein homeostasis under stress.
Photosynthesis and ATP synthesis coupled proton transport are both integral membrane-associated processes.
Signal transduction and response to stress co-regulate innate immunity pathways in rice.
DNA repair and cell division checkpoints are coupled to ensure genomic fidelity after replication.
Both kinase activity (GO:0016301) and DNA repair (GO:0006281) were enriched in heat-stressed samples.
GO:0006950 and GO:0007165 are co-activated in rice roots exposed to cadmium toxicity.
The term GO:0015979 refers to photosynthesis, a key process in plant energy metabolism.
GO:0051301 (cell division) and GO:0071555 (cell wall organization) coordinate during plant development.
RNA-seq data revealed enrichment of GO:0006412 (translation) and GO:0006468 (protein phosphorylation).
GO:0000001 (线粒体遗传) 和 GO:0006281 (DNA修复) 在同一应激信号通路中协作。
氧化磷酸化 (GO:0006119) 和 ATP synthesis coupled proton transport (GO:0015986) 在线粒体中紧密耦合。
叶绿体 (GO:0009507) 是光合作用 (GO:0015979) 的主要发生场所。
水稻中 signal transduction (GO:0007165) 调控 response to stress (GO:0006950) 基因的表达。
GO:0005634 (nucleus) contains the machinery for GO:0006281 (DNA repair) and transcription.
Under drought, protein phosphorylation and signal transduction pathways were enriched in rice flag leaves.
The MAPK cascade triggered kinase activity to amplify stress signaling, ultimately inducing DNA repair.
Mutant plants showed defects in signal transduction, yet chloroplast ultrastructure remained intact.
Chloroplast proteome remodeling supports photosynthetic acclimation to fluctuating light intensity.
The inner mitochondrial membrane houses the respiratory chain driving oxidative phosphorylation and ATP synthesis.
Mitochondrion inheritance is disrupted when cytoskeletal dynamics are perturbed during cell division.
The coordination of translation and protein phosphorylation ensures accurate proteome adaptation to stress.
The nucleus integrates transcriptional programs governing response to stress and cell division timing.
Cell wall organization defects compromise mechanical signal transmission during rapid cell division phases.
Oxidative phosphorylation and transmembrane transport both depend on electrochemical gradients across membranes.
ATP synthesis coupled proton transport was upregulated alongside active cell division in meristems.
The protein kinase OsCPK12 activates DNA repair mechanisms in response to oxidative stress.
Mitochondrial genome maintenance is coordinated with DNA repair to preserve organellar genome stability.
Signal transduction from receptor to nucleus involves sequential kinase phosphorylation events.
Chloroplast biogenesis requires coordinated transmembrane import of nucleus-encoded proteins.
Photosynthesis-derived ATP fuels transmembrane transport of sucrose in phloem loading.
Ribosomal translation and kinase-mediated phosphorylation cooperate to sense nitrogen availability.
Cell wall organization requires controlled transmembrane transport of cell wall precursors.
Mitochondrion inheritance is coupled to cell division via the fission machinery.
Oxidative damage triggers response to stress and activates nuclear DNA repair enzymes.
OsWRKY45 regulates kinase activity and modulates response to blast fungus infection.
Transcriptome analysis indicated elevated cell division coinciding with protein phosphorylation upregulation.
Chloroplast membrane integrity supports photosynthesis and facilitates transmembrane ion transport.
Mitochondrial inner membrane potential is essential for oxidative phosphorylation efficiency.
OsDREB2 modulates DNA repair and stress response under high temperature in rice.
OsMADS51 controls cell division (GO:0051301) and DNA repair (GO:0006281) during floral transition in rice.
Photosynthesis efficiency (GO:0015979) and 跨膜转运 (GO:0055085) are co-regulated by light signals.
GO:0006119 (氧化磷酸化) and kinase activity (GO:0016301) are both elevated in rapidly dividing cells.
Signal transduction (GO:0007165) 调控 chloroplast (GO:0009507) biogenesis under varying light conditions.
GO:0000002 (线粒体基因组维持) and DNA repair (GO:0006281) ensure organellar genome fidelity.
The gene product localizes to the powerhouse organelle inner membrane and participates in the proton gradient-driven synthesis of adenosine triphosphate.
Plants lacking this factor show impaired capacity to replicate organellar genomes and accumulate unrepaired strand breaks, ultimately triggering programmed cell death.
Loss of this nuclear-localized kinase abolishes downstream phosphorylation cascades and sensitizes cells to genotoxic agents.
Depletion of the light-harvesting complex impairs the quantum yield of carbon fixation in illuminated leaf tissue.
The organelle responsible for housing the photosynthetic machinery undergoes structural reorganization under photooxidative conditions.
A rice factor essential for organellar genome replication co-purifies with proteins involved in strand-break rejoining.
Disruption of the proton motive force across the energy-transducing membrane halts the rotary nanomotor driving nucleotide synthesis.
The cytoplasmic messenger relay converts extracellular hormone perception into nuclear transcriptional reprogramming.
Failure to partition the double-stranded genome equally between progeny cells leads to aneuploidy and growth arrest.
The polypeptide-elongating ribosomal machinery is subject to inhibitory phosphorylation under nutrient deprivation.
A transporter embedded in the plastid envelope shuttles carbon skeletons required for amino acid biosynthesis.
Under prolonged darkness, the carbon-assimilating reactions cease and the organelle undergoes etioplast conversion.
Phosphorylation-dependent activation of the guardian kinase initiates a signaling cascade that halts chromosome segregation.
The energy released from electron transfer to molecular oxygen is coupled to the vectorial translocation of protons.
Crosstalk between the organellar DNA surveillance machinery and the cytosolic stress-sensing network modulates adaptive responses.
An RNA-binding chaperone maintains translational fidelity during heat-induced proteotoxic stress in the cytoplasm.
The barrier separating the nuclear compartment from the cytoplasm gates the passage of macromolecular complexes.
Sequential phosphorylation of scaffold proteins amplifies the initial ligand-receptor binding event into a sustained transcriptional response.
A RecQ-family helicase unwinds stalled replication forks in plastid DNA to allow error-free resumption of replication.
Vesicle-mediated delivery of glycan polymers to the growing cell plate coordinates with cytokinetic membrane fusion.
Suppression of the ATP-generating machinery in the inner membrane sensitizes rice plants to chilling-induced cell death.
The photoreceptor-controlled developmental switch upregulates carbon-fixation enzyme abundance in emerging seedling leaves.
Phosphoproteomic mapping revealed hyperphosphorylation of a kinase substrate cluster in UV-damaged cells undergoing genome surveillance.
Fluorescence microscopy demonstrated co-localization of the replication factor with the mitochondrial nucleoid structure.
A long-distance electrical signal propagated through plasmodesmata activates wound-responsive kinase cascades in distal leaves.
The penalty for mispartitioning the endosymbiotic genome is heteroplasmy-driven respiratory deficiency in daughter cells.
Cotranslational folding guided by ribosome-associated chaperones prevents aggregation of nascent polypeptides during stress.
Ion homeostasis across the chloroplast envelope is maintained by a family of antiporters whose activity is light-regulated.
The checkpoint kinase phosphorylates histone H2AX at double-strand break sites, recruiting repair factors to nuclear foci.
Knockdown of the plastid-targeted ribosome recycling factor disrupts organellar translation and chloroplast biogenesis.
The organelle governing oxidative phosphorylation (GO:0006119) distributes asymmetrically — 线粒体遗传 (GO:0000001) ensures copy fidelity.
Phosphorylation cascades (GO:0006468) converging on 细胞核 (GO:0005634) remodel chromatin to permit DNA repair (GO:0006281).
Light-driven charge separation in 叶绿体 drives ATP synthesis coupled proton transport (GO:0015986) supporting carbon fixation.
GO:0051301 (cell division) checkpoints integrate 信号转导 (GO:0007165) and DNA damage sensing to gate mitotic entry.
The inner membrane of 线粒体 (GO:0005743) serves as the platform for both GO:0006119 and GO:0015986 under aerobic conditions.
Translational reprogramming (GO:0006412) triggered by 应激响应 (GO:0006950) selectively upregulates repair factors.
Cell wall organization (GO:0071555) at the 细胞板 requires vesicle delivery via 跨膜转运 (GO:0055085) during cytokinesis.
Kinase activity (GO:0016301) phosphorylates 叶绿体 envelope proteins, regulating GO:0055085 of metabolite precursors.
线粒体基因组维持 (GO:0000002) failure elevates reactive oxygen species, inducing GO:0006950 and GO:0006281 simultaneously.
GO:0015979 (光合作用) generates reductants that feed into 氧化磷酸化 (GO:0006119) via metabolite shuttling across membranes.
Field trials compared planting density, panicle length, and grain weight across ten cultivars in two consecutive growing seasons.
Irrigation scheduling significantly affected tillering rate and final biomass accumulation in paddy rice.
The hybrid variety showed superior yield stability across five agro-ecological zones compared with the inbred check.
Farmers in the Yangtze River Delta reported earlier heading dates and higher milling quality after adopting the new cultivar.
Nitrogen topdressing at panicle initiation increased spikelet number per panicle but reduced filled grain percentage.
Remote sensing indices derived from UAV imagery correlated well with manually measured leaf area index values.
The lodging resistance of the semi-dwarf lines was evaluated by stem diameter, wall thickness, and bending moment.
Grain chalkiness was negatively correlated with slow grain-filling rate during the ripening stage under high night temperature.
A genome-wide association study identified SNPs linked to days-to-heading under both long-day and short-day photoperiods.
Comparative transcriptomic profiling between indica and japonica subspecies highlighted allelic variation at yield QTLs.
Soil organic carbon content and bulk density were the strongest predictors of paddy yield in the Mekong Delta.
Agronomic management practices such as alternate wetting and drying reduced methane emissions without penalizing grain yield.
Molecular marker-assisted selection accelerated the introgression of blast-resistance loci into elite breeding lines.
Post-harvest drying temperature influenced head rice recovery and appearance quality in milled rice samples.
The coefficient of variation for 1000-grain weight was lowest in the flat-seedbed transplanting treatment.
Irrigation scheduling and 播期调整 are the primary agronomic factors affecting final yield stability.
The SNP marker RM5961 is tightly linked to the qGW5 QTL controlling 千粒重 in indica rice populations.
Nitrogen use efficiency (NUE) and 肥料利用率 were significantly improved by controlled-release fertilizer application.
UAV-based 冠层温度 measurements correlated with field-measured 产量 under drought-stress conditions.
头季稻留桩高度 (stubble height) affects ratoon tiller emergence and subsequent 再生季产量.
Hybrid vigor for 穗粒数 and 结实率 was observed across multiple indica/japonica cross combinations.
Grain protein content (GPC) and 整精米率 were negatively correlated across fifty elite breeding lines.
